channel.title: Commonplaces on Justin Hayes
channel.description: Recent content in Commonplaces on Justin Hayes

item.title: Murray's Translation of Iliad Book 24 Lines 46-54
item.description: A man may have lost one dearer even than this man was–a brother, whom the same mother bore, or perhaps a son; yet surely when he has wept and wailed for him he makes an end; for an enduring heart have the Fates given to men. But this man, when he has robbed noble Hector of life, binds him behind his chariot and drags him about the mound of his dear comrade; surely neither honor nor profit will he have of this.
